definition: Animal Services Counselor. Staff position focused on adoption and rescue
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Support Services Technician. Support staff who assist behind the scenes such as facilities or janitorial
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Complaint Against. Who is the complaint about? The person, owner, business, or responsible party that the caller is reporting
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Reporting Party. Who is calling? The person, organization, or agency that is contacting the shelter to report a concern or request service
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Quarantine Release. Animal under state mandated rabies quarantine is released
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Rabies Control Investigation. Also known as bite report
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: A service request or case created in the Chameleon system for field officers or staff to complete (such as responding to a stray animal complaint or conducting a welfare check).
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Field staff who respond to animal-related calls, investigate complaints, and enforce animal laws.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Unique identification number assigned to each animal that enters the RCDAS system.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: California Department of Transportation, responsible for deceased animal removal on state highways and freeways.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Phone system software used for call center operations.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

term: Community cat (aka stray cat or free-roaming cat)
definition: Cats are not required to be licensed or registered in the State of California. Cats that live outdoors, either part of the time or all the time, are considered community cats.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Temporary care for stray animals by volunteers in their own homes instead of bringing them to the shelter, which increases the chances of reuniting with owners.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Incorporated cities that contract with Riverside County Department of Animal Services (RCDAS) to provide animal control field services, animal sheltering services, or both.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: The intersecting streets nearest to a specific address, used to help locate exact positions.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Coachella Valley Animal Campus, one of four RCDAS shelter facilities.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Process of offering alternatives to pet surrender, including resources and support services to help owners keep their animals.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Humane euthanasia services provided to pet owners who cannot afford private veterinary care.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: The humane ending of an animal's life, typically due to severe illness, injury, or behavioral issues.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: A request to extend the deadline for paying an animal control citation.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Wild or untamed animals that avoid human contact, typically referring to cats that were not socialized to humans.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Geographic reference codes used to identify specific locations within the county.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Healthy Pet Zone program providing veterinary care, supplies, and support to help families keep their pets during difficult times.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: The process of receiving and documenting animals brought into the shelter system.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Geographic area served by RCDAS (unincorporated Riverside County plus contracted cities).
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Individual housing unit for animals in the shelter; also refers to the department that manages daily animal care.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Newborn animals, typically referring to puppies or kittens under four weeks of age.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: A formal complaint document required to initiate noise violation proceedings against pet owners.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Door hanger used by ACOs after visiting a property and not making contact with a person.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Out of Jurisdiction - areas not served by RCDAS, requiring referral to other agencies.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: ID number used to identify individuals in the Chameleon database system.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Classification system for field activities based on urgency (1=Emergency, 2=Urgent, 3=Assist Fire/Law, 4=Must be responded to same day, 5=Routine).
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Riverside County Department of Animal Services.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Shelter-Neuter-Return program for friendly stray cats that are spayed/neutered, then adopted or returned to their territory.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Surgical procedures to prevent animal reproduction (spay for females, neuter for males).
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: A fee charged to owners who surrender their pets to the shelter, intended to cover care costs.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Date and time notation added to database records to track when actions occurred.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: A humane cage device used to capture cats for TNR programs safely.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Areas of Riverside County not within city limits, served directly by county government rather than municipal services.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Unique identifier assigned to each citation or violation case.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Investigation to assess whether animals are receiving proper care, food, water, and shelter.
source: RCDAS New Staff Reference Guide (Call Center & Customer Service)

definition: Animal transferred from contract city that has animal control but not sheltering Example: Desert Hot Springs transfers animals to CVAC for sheltering
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred internally between RCDAS shelters (Blythe, CVAC, Jurupa Valley, San Jacinto) Example: Blythe, primarily an intake shelter, transfers animals to CVAC to increase visibility and opportunities for live outcomes
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal custody permanently transferred from RCDAS to an individual adopter at an adoption event.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal adopted unaltered where a s/n deposit has been paid Example: Family adopts a dog or cat that is unaltered and pays a spay/neuter deposit to return for surgery
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: General adoption (most common adoption type) Example: Family adopts dog at RCDAS mega adoption event
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal was adopted and is in shelter awaiting surgery before going home Example: Family adopts a dog or cat and the animal stays in the shelter until surgery
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to Animal Samaritans (animalsamaritans.org) Example: Animals transferred to Animal Samaritan's rescue partner
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to veterinarian for medical care Example: Injured animal sent to offsite emergency veterinary clinic
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred unaltered where a s/n deposit has been paid Example: Unaltered animal transferred pending spay/neuter surgery deposit
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to Animal Friends of The Valley (AFV) Example: Field Officers transferring impounded animals to AFV that were impounded in JPA (Joint Powers Authority)
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to offsite location (not Petsmart of Petco) Example: Animals temporarily displayed at special community event
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to Petsmart location for adoption exposure Example: Adoptable cats transferred to Petsmart adoption center
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal being held for rescue pickup Example: Rescue partner has made a commitment on an animal but the animal is still in the shelter
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to rescue organization Example: Breed-specific rescue takes dog for placement
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal transferred to Mary S. Roberts (MSR) rescue Example: Animal transferred to MSR
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal euthanized while in RCDAS custody.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: Animal died unassisted while in RCDAS custody.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi, Nov 2025)

definition: A city or county-operated entity providing animal control services and housing services. Often named Animal Services or Animal Care and Control. RCDAS is this type of organization.
synonyms: animal services; animal care and control; pound
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A private or nonprofit agency with a formal contract with a government entity for animal control or housing services.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A private or nonprofit agency with a physical facility but no government contract.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Nonprofit organizations that may or may not have government contracts. Traditionally unaffiliated with national organizations of similar names.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Also called foster-based rescue. An animal welfare organization that houses animals exclusively in the homes of volunteers or staff. May be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and can hold full or partial government contracts.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A nonprofit that houses animals for the remainder of their lives. Some sanctuaries also function as rescue groups that adopt some animals out.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: An agency providing direct or partnered services without sheltering animals.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A process where the organization conducts an intake assessment of a stray pet with its finder. The pet stays with the finder for the agreed hold period, preventing shelter overcrowding while keeping the pet in a home environment.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Total animals entering the organization, including transfers in from other organizations.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Total animals entering the shelter minus transfers in from other organizations. Prevents double-counting when summing intakes across multiple organizations.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: SAC term for an animal admitted by its owner, including adoption returns. RCDAS records this as OWNER SUR.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Pets impounded by animal control or law enforcement due to neglect, cruelty, ordinance violations, police arrests, evictions, or other legal circumstances. RCDAS records this as CONFISCATE.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Animal transferred into the organization's possession from another organization.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Sum of animals that left through a live outcome type minus transfers to another animal welfare organization.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Sum of animals that left via a non-live outcome: died, missing, and euthanized.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: All outcomes: adoptions, return to owner or field, other live outcomes, transfers out, shelter euthanasia, and died or lost in care.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Animal custody transitioned to another organization, such as local rescue partners or national groups.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Animal died, unassisted, while in the custody of the organization. RCDAS records this as DIED.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: Animal euthanized in the custody of the organization.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: An owner brings their pet specifically requesting euthanasia due to a medical or behavioral issue. SAC recommends treating OIE as a community service rather than an intake and outcome. RCDAS records this as EUTH REQ.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A pet found by animal control or shelter staff in the community and returned to its owner without entering the organization or being processed as an intake. SAC reports this as a community service.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The number of animals in care on the first day of the selected period.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The number of animals in care on the last day of the selected period.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The percentage of total intakes that are a specific intake type. Calculated as intake type divided by total gross intakes.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The percentage of total outcomes that are a specific outcome type. Calculated as outcome type divided by total outcomes.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The animal's reproductive status. Also called sterilized/unsterilized or spayed/neutered/intact. May be updated during the stay.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A more specific description of why the animal is in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported reason the animal is in the organization's custody. Possible reasons vary widely among shelters.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A more specific description of why the animal is no longer in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported reason the animal is no longer in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The animal's primary and, if applicable, secondary breed, usually determined only by physical appearance and a breed guess.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported or estimated birthdate of the animal, often a guess based on appearance and teeth.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The animal's age group at intake.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A collaborative, industry-led nonprofit that maintains The National Database of sheltered animal statistics, used for trends and benchmarking across the country. RCDAS reports data to SAC.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)
